fix(scheduler): use Asia/Bangkok (GMT+7) for report time matching

VPS runs UTC — all time functions were comparing against UTC clock.
User sets reportTime in Thai time (GMT+7), so scheduler was off by 7h.

- getThaiParts(): Intl.DateTimeFormat with timeZone='Asia/Bangkok'
- getCurrentHHMM(): Thai HH:MM for reportTime matching
- getCurrentDayOfWeek(): Thai day-of-week (1=Mon..7=Sun)
- getTodayKey(): Thai YYYY-MM-DD (fixes UTC midnight day-boundary bug)
- composeReport date header: now shows Thai date correctly

commit.message:
Add token-based chunking and Candle embedding backend

Token-based chunking: Introduce TokenBudget abstraction (Characters or
Tokens variants) injected into all chunkers at construction time. When a
tokenizer is available, chunks are sized to 80% of the model's context
window (e.g. 1638 tokens for EmbeddingGemma 2048). Falls back to
character-based sizing when no model is loaded. Fixes latent bug where
IndexerSettings.max_chunk_size was never propagated to chunkers.

Candle backend: Add CandleEmbedder with full NomicBERT implementation
(rotary embeddings, gated MLP, mean pooling, Matryoshka truncation).
Backend auto-selected by model file extension (.safetensors → Candle,
.onnx → ONNX). Unified behind Embed trait via Embedder enum. Adds
Metal GPU acceleration on macOS.

Also: propagate embedding dimensions through StoreConfig to correctly
size the vector index for any model.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add MITM proxy mode to sniff real inverter Modbus traffic

When mitm_mode.enabled=true, the addon becomes a transparent TCP proxy:
- Listens on the configured port (default 5020)
- Forwards all Nibe requests to the real inverter (upstream_host:upstream_port)
- Rewrites unit IDs (nibe→inverter on request, inverter→nibe on response)
- Logs every register read and response at INFO level

Use to capture exactly which registers Nibe polls from a recognized inverter.
Config: mitm_mode.upstream_host=192.168.68.79, upstream_port=502, upstream_unit_id=3

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Classify DriveStrengthWeak0Weak1 → §6.7.1 [skip ci]

The test parses `wire (weak0, weak1) w;` — a net declaration with a drive strength specification using built-in net types. LRM §6.7.1 ("Net declarations with built-in net types") governs this construct; Syntax 6-2 in that subclause defines drive_strength with strength0 ::= supply0 | strong0 | pull0 | weak0 and strength1 ::= supply1 | strong1 | pull1 | weak1. No finer subclause exists within 6.7.1 for drive strength on net declarations.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Reduce stream latency with low-latency FFmpeg and HLS.js tuning

FFmpeg: add -fflags nobuffer, -flags low_delay, split_by_time
Config: reduce segment time 2s→1s, playlist size 5→3
HLS.js: enable lowLatencyMode, liveSyncDurationCount=1,
        minimal back buffer and forward buffer

Expected latency improvement from ~8-12s to ~2-4s.

Fix tail scaling: only scale root bone to prevent compounding

Scaling all 5 tail bones caused exponential compounding through the
bone chain (child inherits parent scale). Now only Tail_B1_040 is
scaled, which uniformly affects the entire tail without accumulation.

[fix] Python entry point が sys.argv を使うよう修正 (#46)

OS がシェバン経由でエントリーポイントスクリプトを実行すると
  python3 /path/.venv/bin/mille check
という形になり、Rust の std::env::args() は
  ["python3", "/path/.venv/bin/mille", "check"]
を受け取る。clap はスクリプトパスをサブコマンドと誤認して失敗する。

Python の sys.argv はインタープリタが
  ["/path/.venv/bin/mille", "check"]
に設定済みなので、_main() でそちらを run_cli_from() に渡す。

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix 6 bugs found in codebase audit

Bug fixes:
- report.py + llm_overlay.py: wrong breadth key names (pct_above_ma50 ->
  pct_above_200ma, spy_above_ma50 -> spy_above_200ma); report always showed
  '?' for crypto and equity breadth values; labels updated to MA200 as well
- daily_runner.py: BTC volatility halt gate used key 'BTCUSDT' but assets
  are keyed by display name 'BTC'; halt gate was silently failing on every run
- correlation.py: added minimum baseline guard; baseline slice now requires
  >= 2x corr_window valid bars before computing z-score to avoid noisy stats
  from insufficient history
- allocator.py: added MAX_SINGLE_WEIGHT = 20% hard concentration cap per
  asset; prevents full regime cap going into a single position when only 1
  asset qualifies (e.g. only TLT passes in current bear market)
- risk_shield.py + paper_runner.py: added drawdown recovery hysteresis;
  once the 8% halve-exposure rule fires it stays active until drawdown
  recovers below 6% (DRAWDOWN_RECOVERY_THRESHOLD), preventing whipsaw
  rebalancing when drawdown hovers near the trigger level

All 138 tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Adds scripts/release.sh and `make release VERSION=x.y.z` for cutting
manual releases when features warrant a specific semver (e.g., minor

CHANGELOG.md, commits, tags, and pushes — triggering the existing
build and release workflows.

Weekly cron builds continue to handle routine patch bumps automatically.

- features.ts: import swap whale-listener → whale-monitor
- config.ts: add minCopyScore (55), copyLatencyBudgetMs (2000)
- server.ts: import swap, scanner → whale-monitor references
- index.ts: rewire boot sequence, add fetchStrikePrice 15s periodic task
- types.ts: WhaleCopyMeta interface, WhaleSignal.txHash/detectedAt
- decisions-log.ts: whale signal fields in log entries
